prompts.title: Taj Mahal 45-Minute Walking Commentary
prompts.prompt: Act as an MoT-approved Agra tour guide with 14 years on the Taj Mahal circuit. Write a 45-minute walking commentary script split across: (1) main entrance (5 min — Mughal architecture context), (2) Charbagh + Yamuna view (8 min — symbolism), (3) main mausoleum exterior (12 min — pietra dura, calligraphy, Quranic verses respectfully), (4) interior (8 min — cenotaph, marble work), (5) mosque + jawab (6 min — symmetry), (6) end at exit + Q&A buffer (6 min). Each section: scripted talk + cultural-respect notes + 1 anecdote + 1 photo-op pause. Constraints: respect ASI rules (no shoes, no flash); cite Aga Khan Trust restoration; bilingual fallback; group of 12.

prompts.title: Cultural Etiquette Briefing for Foreign Tourists
prompts.prompt: Act as an inbound tour director. Write a 350-word cultural briefing for first-time foreign tourists landing in India. Cover: (1) greetings (namaste vs handshake), (2) temple etiquette (shoes, phone, women dress), (3) food culture (eating with right hand, prasad, vegetarian options), (4) tipping norms, (5) dress code in conservative areas, (6) photography permissions, (7) currency + UPI usage, (8) avoiding scams (overcharging tuk-tuks, gem fraud). Constraints: warm + non-condescending; respect tourist's dignity; offer Hindi greeting glossary; INR conversions for context; mention 1363 emergency tourist helpline.

prompts.title: Group Management Tips for Heritage Walk
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior city tour guide with 10 years in Delhi heritage walks. Write a guide on managing a 25-pax mixed-age group on a 3-hour Old Delhi walking tour (Jama Masjid - Chandni Chowk - Lal Qila). Cover: (1) start-of-tour roll call + WhatsApp group, (2) pace management for seniors + children, (3) frequent water + bio breaks, (4) safety at busy lanes (traffic, pickpockets), (5) photography spots vs conservative areas, (6) food tasting hygiene tips (where to eat, where to skip), (7) Hindi-speaking buddy assignment for solo non-Hindi tourists, (8) end-of-tour feedback + tip etiquette. Constraints: respect Jama Masjid prayer times; recommend chai stops; backup taxi for senior emergency.

prompts.title: Multilingual Greetings + Cultural Phrases
prompts.prompt: Act as an MoT-trained inbound guide. Build a multilingual greeting + survival-phrase pocket guide for Indian guides serving Spanish, French, German, Japanese, Mandarin, Russian tourists. For each language provide: (1) hello / thank you / please / sorry / yes / no, (2) 'this way' / 'wait here' / 'safe' / 'be careful', (3) numbers 1-10, (4) common food allergens (vegetarian, no spice, no nuts, no dairy), (5) emergency: 'I need a doctor', (6) appreciation phrase. Constraints: phonetic transcription for Indian-tongue speakers; printable A6 pocket card; train-by-rote in one week.

prompts.title: Hampi Ancient Site Commentary
prompts.prompt: Act as an MoT regional guide trained on Karnataka heritage. Write a 60-minute commentary for the Hampi ruins covering: (1) Vijayanagar Empire context (12 min), (2) Virupaksha temple (12 min — alive ritual + history), (3) Vitthala temple complex + stone chariot (15 min — musical pillars story), (4) royal enclosure + queen's bath (12 min), (5) closing reflection at Matanga Hill viewpoint (9 min). Each section: respectful narration + dynastic chronology + decline lesson + 1 fact-check disclaimer (myth vs history). Constraints: align with ASI signage; respect active worship; cite Robert Sewell + recent archaeology; bilingual (English + Kannada / Hindi).

prompts.title: Senior-Pilgrim Char Dham Day Plan
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior-pilgrim Char Dham guide. Write a day-by-day commentary + care plan for a 16-pax 60+ Marathi-speaking pilgrim group on the 12-day Char Dham yatra. Each day: (1) morning briefing, (2) altitude + breathing reminder, (3) medication schedule check, (4) pony / palanquin coordination, (5) aarti darshan timing, (6) meal + hydration plan, (7) night rest + emergency-contact reminder. Constraints: respect AMS risk above 3,000m; bilingual Marathi/Hindi; helicopter option pre-booked; nearest hospital + ALS ambulance protocol.

prompts.title: Conservation-Sensitive Wildlife Tour
prompts.prompt: Act as a Ranthambore safari naturalist with 9 years. Build a 4-hour jeep-safari commentary for an 8-pax tourist group covering: (1) tiger ecology + Project Tiger context (15 min), (2) prey-base education (chital, sambhar, nilgai), (3) bird checklist commentary, (4) responsible-wildlife-photography ethics (no calls, no flash, no chasing), (5) sustainable tourism reminders, (6) what to do on a tiger sighting (silent, no standing, no flash, jeep distance). Constraints: align with NTCA + WPA 1972; respect tiger stress; cite tagging + monitoring science; brand: educate, not entertain.

prompts.title: Backwater Houseboat Cultural Briefing
prompts.prompt: Act as a Kerala backwater tour guide. Write a 30-min welcome briefing on board a houseboat from Alleppey to Kumarakom for a 6-pax international family. Cover: (1) house-boat history (kettuvallam) + craftsmanship, (2) safety briefing (lifejackets, no swimming, child supervision), (3) meal preview (Kerala thali), (4) Toddy-tapping demo (optional + responsible), (5) photo-op stops, (6) sunset cruise vs sunrise option, (7) responsible tourism (no plastic, no loud music), (8) Q&A buffer. Constraints: respect coir + fishing community livelihood; share stories without exoticising; bilingual greetings (Malayalam + English); pricing transparency.

prompts.title: Religious Site Commentary (Varanasi)
prompts.prompt: Act as a Varanasi cultural guide. Write a sensitive 50-minute Ganga Aarti viewing commentary for a 14-pax mixed group (Indian + foreign). Cover: (1) Varanasi-as-sacred-city context (10 min), (2) ghat hierarchy + significance (10 min), (3) cremation philosophy (8 min — handle with maximum dignity, no photos near pyres), (4) Aarti ritual narration (12 min), (5) post-Aarti reflection at boat (10 min). Constraints: respect grieving families (10m+ distance); no photography of cremation; sensitive vocabulary (avoid 'death tourism'); cite ancient Sanskrit + modern philosophy; bilingual.

prompts.title: Food Walk Stop-by-Stop Script
prompts.prompt: Act as a food-walk operator (think Delhi Food Walks / Mumbai Tiffin Trail). Build a 3-hour food-walk script through Old Lucknow (Aminabad - Chowk - Akbari Gate). 8 stops: (1) tunday kababi, (2) prakash ki kulfi, (3) nahum's bakery, (4) ram asrey, (5) raja thandai, (6) sheermal at akbari gate, (7) galouti at Wahid, (8) paan ke deewane. For each: (a) 4-min context (history + family ownership), (b) 1 must-try item with INR price, (c) hygiene check note, (d) photo etiquette, (e) shop owner's name to greet, (f) walking distance to next stop. Constraints: align with FSSAI street-food norms; pace 6 min walking + 14 min eating; respect Ramzan timings.

prompts.title: Conflict Resolution During Tour
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior tour director. Write a 1-page playbook for handling 5 common in-tour conflicts: (1) tourist arrives drunk, (2) two guests disagree publicly, (3) child gets lost in market, (4) tourist refuses to follow safety rules, (5) tourist accuses local vendor of cheating. For each: (a) immediate verbal de-escalation, (b) options to offer, (c) when to involve manager / police / consulate, (d) document for incident report, (e) post-tour follow-up. Constraints: align with TAFI / IATO grievance norms; respect dignity of all parties; preserve tour for other guests; protect Indian local relationships.

prompts.title: Culture-Specific Adaptations (Japanese Group)
prompts.prompt: Act as an inbound guide trained on Japanese tourist preferences. Adapt my standard 8-day Golden Triangle tour for a 14-pax Japanese group. Cover adaptations: (1) punctuality + schedule precision, (2) explanation depth (history + numbers + context), (3) photo-stop rhythm, (4) food preferences (less spice, courteous portions, no public sneezing during meals), (5) shopping interests (silk, jewellery, miniature paintings), (6) communication tone (polite, less casual), (7) bowing + business-card etiquette, (8) JTB / HIS partner liaison. Constraints: respect Japanese cultural sensitivity; minimum English + basic Japanese phrases; group chemistry over individualism; cite Japan Tourist Board norms.

prompts.title: Historical Site Storytelling Framework
prompts.prompt: Act as a heritage storyteller (Sahapedia / Storytrails school). Build a 5-minute storytelling framework for any historical site. Use: (1) hook (a sensory or emotional opening tied to the present moment), (2) historical context (1 minute, with named characters), (3) climactic moment (with detail), (4) modern relevance (why it matters today), (5) reflection prompt (1 question for guests to ponder). Constraints: storytelling > lecturing; respect multiple narratives; cite primary + secondary sources; avoid hyper-nationalist framings; train guides to deliver in ≤300 words.

prompts.title: Tipping + Income Disclosure for Guides
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior MoT-approved guide trainer. Write a 1-page guide on professional tipping + income disclosure. Cover: (1) how to communicate tipping etiquette without expectation (post-tour, never pre-tour), (2) what is a fair tip (5-10% of tour fee), (3) tax implications for guide (ITR-3 / ITR-4 + GST registration if turnover crosses), (4) acceptance modes (UPI / cash), (5) handling tipping disparity in groups, (6) preserving dignity if tip declined, (7) channelising into welfare fund (community guides). Constraints: align with Income Tax Act + GST; transparent + ethical; respect dignity of low-tip days; cite TAFI guideline if available.

prompts.title: MoT Regional-Level Guide Exam Prep
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior MoT trainer. Build a study plan for MoT Regional-Level Guide exam (administered by IITTM). Cover: (1) exam structure (written + oral), (2) syllabus weight (Indian history, art-architecture, world history, geography, etiquette, tourism business, cross-cultural communication), (3) recommended books (NCERT + Stewart Gordon, AL Basham, Coomaraswamy), (4) 90-day study plan, (5) field-visit practice (4 monuments minimum), (6) language assessment (English + 1 foreign / 1 Indian), (7) mock-interview prep, (8) ID + license process post-clearing. Constraints: align with MoT 2024 guide-training framework + IITTM Gwalior; respect 30+ age limit per recent norms; ITDC empanelment after license.
